title: Thermoelectric Mini Fridge
subtitle: 45L Peltier Module Based Portable Cooling System
category: Tier-2 | Embedded Electronics & Thermal Engineering Project
shortDescription: A completed Tier-2 engineering project that demonstrates a 45-liter thermoelectric refrigerator built using dual Peltier modules, forced-air cooling, aluminum heat sinks, and a high-current power supply to study semiconductor-based refrigeration without conventional compressors.
fullDescription: The Thermoelectric Mini Fridge is a completed Tier-2 engineering project developed to understand semiconductor-based refrigeration using the Peltier effect. The system was designed as a compact 45-liter portable refrigerator powered by a 12V 15A switched-mode power supply and two thermoelectric Peltier modules. Large aluminum heat sinks and high-speed cooling fans were used on both the hot and cold sides to improve heat transfer efficiency and maintain continuous cooling performance. The project involved designing the cooling chamber, thermal insulation, airflow management, electrical wiring, and mechanical assembly while evaluating the cooling capability of thermoelectric refrigeration as an alternative to conventional compressor-based cooling systems. This project strengthened practical knowledge in power electronics, thermal management, embedded electronics, and prototype fabrication.
problemStatement: Conventional refrigerators depend on compressor-based vapor compression systems, which are bulky, mechanically complex, noisy, and require refrigerant gases. For small portable cooling applications, an inexpensive solid-state cooling solution can provide an educational platform to study thermal management, semiconductor cooling, and compact refrigeration systems.
solution: This project implements a portable thermoelectric refrigerator using two Peltier modules powered by a 12V 15A SMPS. The cooling system transfers heat from the insulated chamber to the external environment using aluminum heat sinks and forced-air cooling fans. The prototype demonstrates compressor-free refrigeration while providing practical understanding of thermoelectric cooling, power distribution, heat dissipation, insulation techniques, and portable cooling system design.
innovation: Portable refrigeration using forced-air heat dissipation
Educational demonstration of thermoelectric cooling principles
futureWork: Improve insulation for higher thermal efficiency
Add battery backup for portable operation
Optimize airflow using CFD-based thermal design

milestones.title: Project Planning
milestones.description: Studied thermoelectric refrigeration principles, selected Peltier modules, and designed the overall cooling system.

milestones.title: Mechanical Fabrication
milestones.description: Constructed the insulated refrigerator body, mounted heat sinks, cooling fans, and prepared the cooling chamber.

milestones.title: Electrical Integration
milestones.description: Integrated the dual Peltier modules, 12V 15A SMPS, cooling fans, wiring, and complete power distribution.

milestones.title: Performance Testing
milestones.description: Evaluated cooling performance, airflow, heat dissipation, and overall thermal efficiency under continuous operation.

milestones.title: Completed Tier-2 Project
milestones.description: Successfully completed the portable 45-liter thermoelectric refrigerator, demonstrating compressor-free cooling using dual Peltier modules and forced-air thermal management.
